steps.title: Identify what comes before and after the blank
steps.content: Before the blank: “Not all plants benefit equally or from the same kind of [mycorrhizae]” — a complete independent clause making a general claim. After the blank: three examples separated by semicolons (alfalfa/forsythia, tree species, Ericaceae plants) that illustrate exactly how plants differ. When a complete independent clause is followed by a list of illustrating examples, the correct punctuation is a colon.

steps.title: Test each punctuation mark
steps.content:
• Choice A — mycorrhizae;: A semicolon signals two parallel independent clauses of equal weight. The right side is an elaboration, not a parallel thought, and what follows is a series, not a single clause. ✗
  • Choice B — mycorrhizae: No punctuation runs the independent clause directly into the examples — a run-on. ✗
  • Choice C — mycorrhizae,: A comma between an independent clause and the list of examples that follows it creates a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — mycorrhizae:: The colon follows the complete independent clause and correctly introduces the three illustrating examples. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
